steps.title: Use the discriminant condition for one real solution
steps.content:
A quadratic equation $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$ has exactly one real solution when its discriminant equals zero.

In this equation: $a = 1$, $b = \sqrt{k-3}$, $c = 33$.

steps.title: Solve the discriminant equation for k
steps.content:
Substitute and solve:
$$(\sqrt{k-3})^2 - 4(1)(33) = 0$$
$$k - 3 - 132 = 0$$
$$k = 135$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
